articles.headline: Portland visits Washington after Iriafen's 25-point outing

articles.headline: Mystics power past short-handed Tempo 79-62 behind double-doubles by Iriafen and Austin
articles.description: — Kiki Iriafen had 25 points and 14 rebounds, Shakira Austin added 17 points and 10 rebounds, and the Washington Mystics beat the Toronto Tempo 79-62 on Tuesday night.

articles.headline: Man fired after alleged racist message to Aces' Chelsea Gray
articles.description: Hilton Grand Vacations said Tuesday that it has fired a man who allegedly sent a racist message to Aces guard Chelsea Gray on social media.

articles.headline: Aaliyah Edwards and Brittney Griner each score 20-plus to lead the Sun past the Fire 90-87
articles.description: — Aaliyah Edwards scored 21 points off the bench, Brittney Griner added 20, and the Connecticut Sun beat the Portland Fire 90-87 on Tuesday.

articles.headline: Paige Bueckers bemoans WNBA's lack of Black women head coaches
articles.description: Wings star Paige Bueckers called out the lack of Black women head coaches in the WNBA, saying Black women deserve "the same equal opportunity" to coach in the league.

images.name: Paige Bueckers [1296x729]
images.caption: Paige Bueckers is averaging 19.8 points and 5.8 assists but is day-to-day because of a right ankle injury and missed Saturday's loss at Portland.

images.name: Paige Bueckers powers Wings to comeback win with 22 points

articles.headline: WNBA Power Rankings: The Indiana Fever are running hot
articles.description: With Kelsey Mitchell posting her sixth straight game with 25 points or more, Indiana moves up to No. 4. Golden State holds on to the top spot.

articles.headline: Los Angeles visits Minnesota following McBride's 37-point game
articles.description: Los Angeles Sparks (10-12, 5-6 Western Conference) at Minnesota Lynx (18-6, 11-1 Western Conference)

articles.headline: Lynx rookie Miles reaches 400 points, 100 rebounds, 100 assists
articles.description: Minnesota rookie Olivia Miles scored a career-high 33, becoming the first WNBA player to reach 400 points, 100 rebounds and 100 assists in only 22 career games, and the Lynx defeated the Phoenix Mercury 104-100 Monday night for a third straight victory.

images.name: Olivia Miles scores career-high 33 points in Lynx's win over the Mercury
images.caption: Olivia Miles scores career-high 33 points in Lynx's win over the Mercury

articles.headline: Kayla McBride, Olivia Miles star in the Lynx's 104-100 win over the Mercury
articles.description: — Kayla McBride scored a season-high 37 points, rookie Olivia Miles had a career-high 33 and the Minnesota Lynx beat the Phoenix Mercury 104-100 on Monday night for their third straight victory.

article.headline: Angel Reese secures 64th career double-double and the Dream beat the Storm 89-78 to end 5-game skid
article.description: — Allisha Gray scored 22 points, Angel Reese had 18 points and 11 rebounds for her league-best 15th double-double of the season, and the Atlanta Dream beat the Seattle Storm 89-78 on Thursday night to end a five-game losing streak.
article.linkText: Angel Reese secures 64th career double-double and the Dream beat the Storm 89-78 to end 5-game skid

tracking.trackingName: WNBA_One-Play (Atlanta Dream vs. Seattle Storm - Game Highlights) 2026/07/10 ESHEET

article.story: COLLEGE PARK, Ga. -- — Allisha Gray scored 22 points, Angel Reese had 18 points and 11 rebounds for her league-best 15th double-double of the season, and the Atlanta Dream beat the Seattle Storm 89-78 on Thursday night to end a five-game losing streak. Reese passed Tina Charles (63) for the most double-doubles in a player’s first three WNBA seasons. She was helped off the court with 1:19 remaining after rolling her right ankle. Reese put the Dream ahead 68-48 midway through the third quarter, and they led by at least eight points throughout in the fourth. Natisha Hiedeman led Seattle (6-18) with 20 points and Dominique Malonga had 15 points and nine rebounds. Flau’jae Johnson finished with 12 points. Seattle had a season-high 21 turnovers. Rhyne Howard added 19 points for Atlanta (13-9), which won for the first time since June 22. Howard went 4 of 11 from 3-point range, while Seattle shot 3 for 20. The Dream had 14-plus steals for the third straight game. LSU women's basketball coach Kim Mulkey was in attendance to see former players Reese and Johnson. Up next Storm: At Washington on Sunday. Dream: Host Portland on Saturday. ------ AP WNBA: https://apnews.com/hub/wnba-basketball
